text: how to properly focus histology slides and identify key structures. • Demonstrate an adequate understand of the material in this section. BACKGROUND A solid foundation is essential when learning any new skill. Understanding anatomical directions, articulations, planes, and regions are the foundation for learning anatomy. The standard anatomical position of the human body is facing towards the observer, legs hip- width apart, feet facing forward, arms out slightly at either side with palms facing forward. When determining a structure’s relative position, be sure to use this frame of reference. For example, it can be easy to confuse which side is the anterior aspect of the hands, therefore, one might incorrectly assume that the thumb is medial to the little finger.
